perType.type: grunt — overhead club smash (club is parented to arms.R fist, so the arm swing carries it). dur 1.05s. ATK_HITFRAC ~0.55. Owns arms.L, arms.R, torso, head, g.scale.y(slight), lunge.
perType.windup: u 0..0.40 (easeInOut on w=remap(u,0,0.40)): coil back & up. arms.R.rotation.x = lerp(0.5, -1.4, w) (club rears overhead-back); arms.R.rotation.z = lerp(0,-0.25,w); arms.L.rotation.x = lerp(0.5,-0.5,w) (off-hand rises for balance); torso.rotation.x = lerp(0,-0.30,w) (lean back); head.rotation.x = lerp(0,-0.20,w) (look up at the raise); slight crouch g.scale.y = m.scale*lerp(1,0.97,w); lunge(lerp(0,-0.15,w)) (rock back 0.15m).
perType.strike: u 0.40..0.62 (s=easeOutBack(remap)): explosive overhead chop with overshoot. arms.R.rotation.x = lerp(-1.4, 1.85, s) (blows PAST 1.6 then settles via easeOutBack); arms.R.rotation.z = lerp(-0.25,0,s); arms.L.rotation.x = lerp(-0.5,0.9,s); torso.rotation.x = lerp(-0.30,0.45,s) (whole body drives down behind the club); head.rotation.x = lerp(-0.20,0.35,s); g.scale.y = m.scale*lerp(0.97,1.0,s); lunge(lerp(-0.15,0.30,s)) — onHit/damagePlayer fires at u>=0.55, the impact frame.
perType.recovery: u 0.62..1.0 (r=easeOutCubic(remap)): heavy settle to walk baseline. arms.R.rotation.x = lerp(1.85,0.5,r); arms.R.rotation.z = lerp(0,0,r); arms.L.rotation.x = lerp(0.9,0.5,r); torso.rotation.x = lerp(0.45,0,r); head.rotation.x = lerp(0.35,0,r); g.scale.y -> m.scale; lunge held then lunge(0) at endAtk.
perType.lunge: rock back 0.15m on windup, drive +0.30m toward player on the strike frame, restored to lungeBase on recovery end. Sells the committed downward chop weight.

perType.type: scout — fast twin-claw double swipe (bone daggers on both forearms). dur 0.42s, no anticipation (twitchy predator). ATK_HITFRAC ~0.30 (first swipe lands). Owns arms.L, arms.R, torso(slight twist), legs(hop), g.scale, lunge.
perType.windup: minimal — u 0..0.08 (w=remap): a 1-frame snap-load only. arms.R.rotation.x = lerp(0.5,-0.5,w); arms.L.rotation.x = lerp(0.5,0.2,w). No torso lean, no back-step (it's a pounce, not a wind-up).
perType.strike: TWO alternating swipes inside u 0.08..0.65. Swipe A (s1=easeOutBack(remap(u,0.08,0.34))): arms.R.rotation.x = lerp(-0.5,1.4,s1) + arms.R.rotation.z = lerp(0,0.5,s1) (cross-body slash); arms.L.rotation.x = lerp(0.2,-0.6,s1) (winds for swipe B); torso.rotation.z = lerp(0,0.2,s1). Swipe B (s2=easeOutBack(remap(u,0.34,0.65))): arms.L.rotation.x = lerp(-0.6,1.4,s2) + arms.L.rotation.z = lerp(0,-0.5,s2); arms.R.rotation.x = lerp(1.4,0.3,s2); torso.rotation.z = lerp(0.2,-0.2,s2). A small hop: g.scale.y = m.scale*(1+0.05*Math.sin(u*Math.PI)) and lunge(0.18*Math.sin(u*Math.PI)) (lunge in and back out within the swing). onHit fires once at u>=0.30 (single damage event, between the two visual swipes).
perType.recovery: u 0.65..1.0 (r=easeOutCubic(remap)): quick snap back to guard. arms.L.rotation.x->0.5, arms.L.rotation.z->0, arms.R.rotation.x->0.5, arms.R.rotation.z->0, torso.rotation.z->0 all via lerp(cur,target,r); lunge(0) at end. Fast recovery so it can re-engage immediately.
perType.lunge: a quick in-and-out hop (0 -> +0.18m -> 0 across the swing via sin), paired with a 5% scale.y pop. Light & darting, NOT a heavy drop.

perType.type: brute — heavy two-fist ground slam (giant maul gripped on g; both arms drive). dur 1.5s (longest, ponderous). ATK_HITFRAC ~0.50. Owns arms.L, arms.R, torso, head, legs(crouch), g.scale, lunge. Pairs with the EXISTING knockback/shake/rooted/gore block already in the brute onHit.
perType.windup: LONG coil — u 0..0.45 (w=easeInOut(remap)): both arms rear up, head down, body squashes to load. arms.L.rotation.x = lerp(0.5,-1.6,w); arms.R.rotation.x = lerp(0.5,-1.6,w); arms.L.rotation.z=lerp(0,0.2,w); arms.R.rotation.z=lerp(0,-0.2,w); torso.rotation.x = lerp(0,-0.25,w); head.rotation.x = lerp(0,0.30,w) (head tilts DOWN to target the ground); legs.L.rotation.x=legs.R.rotation.x=lerp(0,0.35,w) (crouch); SQUASH g.scale.set(m.scale*(1+0.06*w), m.scale*(1-0.12*w), m.scale*(1+0.06*w)) (coils down & wide ~0.88 y); lunge(lerp(0,-0.10,w)).
perType.strike: u 0.45..0.60 (s=easeOutBack(remap)): both fists SLAM down + stretch. arms.L.rotation.x = lerp(-1.6,1.95,s); arms.R.rotation.x = lerp(-1.6,1.95,s) (overshoot past 1.9); arms.z -> 0; torso.rotation.x = lerp(-0.25,0.40,s); head.rotation.x stays down ~0.35; legs uncoil legs.x=lerp(0.35,0,s); STRETCH g.scale.set(m.scale*(1-0.04*s), m.scale*(1+0.04*s), m.scale*(1-0.04*s)) then settle; lunge(lerp(-0.10,0.28,s)). onHit at u>=0.50 fires the EXISTING brute hit (damage + kb 3.2 + clampToBoundary + shake 0.5 + m.rooted 0.5 + gore.burst) — the shake/knockback IS the impact emphasis.
perType.recovery: u 0.60..1.0 (r=easeOutCubic(remap)): slow heavy recovery. arms.L/R.rotation.x = lerp(1.95,0.5,r); torso.rotation.x = lerp(0.40,0,r); head.rotation.x = lerp(0.35,0,r); g.scale -> m.scale (uniform) via lerp each axis; lunge(0) at end. Deliberately the slowest recovery of all types (ponderous personality).
perType.lunge: small rock-back 0.10m on the long coil, heavy +0.28m drop-forward on the slam, restored on recovery. The scale squash->stretch carries most of the weight; the lunge + existing screen shake/knockback finish it.

perType.type: shard — quick jab (single short straight punch, fastest melee). dur 0.30s, near-zero windup. ATK_HITFRAC ~0.45. Owns arms.R, torso(slight), lunge. Replaces the generic default-case meleeAttack.
perType.windup: u 0..0.15 (w=remap, linear is fine it's so short): tiny chamber. arms.R.rotation.x = lerp(0.5,0.0,w) (pull the fist back to chest, NOT overhead); torso.rotation.z = lerp(0,0.12,w) (cock the shoulder).
perType.strike: u 0.15..0.45 (s=easeOutBack(remap)): short sharp straight thrust. arms.R.rotation.x = lerp(0.0,1.5,s) (punches out forward, slight overshoot); torso.rotation.z = lerp(0.12,-0.08,s) (shoulder follows the jab); lunge(lerp(0,0.20,s)). onHit at u>=0.45.
perType.recovery: u 0.45..1.0 (r=easeOutCubic(remap)): retract to guard quickly. arms.R.rotation.x = lerp(1.5,0.5,r); torso.rotation.z = lerp(-0.08,0,r); lunge(0) at end. Snappy because shard is rubble-fast and fragile.
perType.lunge: a small +0.20m forward jab-step on the strike only, restored on recovery. No scale change (light, brittle).

perType.type: wisp — darting lunge-bite (FAST spirit rush; no weapon, the body IS the attack). dur 0.45s. ATK_HITFRAC ~0.45. Owns torso, head, arms(swept back), g.scale, lunge. Drive it through the new default/melee path with kind 'wispBite'. Composes with its hover/bob locomotion (see resetRule note).
perType.windup: u 0..0.20 (w=easeInOut(remap)): coil/inhale — pull the body back & compress like a spring. torso.rotation.x = lerp(0,-0.35,w) (rear back); head.rotation.x = lerp(0,-0.25,w); arms.L.rotation.x=arms.R.rotation.x=lerp(0.5,-0.2,w) (arms sweep back like trailing flame); g.scale.set(m.scale*(1-0.06*w), m.scale*(1+0.10*w), m.scale*(1-0.06*w)) (stretch tall = charging); lunge(lerp(0,-0.20,w)) (recoil back 0.2m).
perType.strike: u 0.20..0.50 (s=easeOutBack(remap)): whip-forward bite/headbutt. torso.rotation.x = lerp(-0.35,0.55,s) (lunge the whole body forward); head.rotation.x = lerp(-0.25,0.45,s) (the bite); arms.x = lerp(-0.2,1.2,s) (claws sweep forward); g.scale.set(m.scale*(1+0.08*s), m.scale*(1-0.06*s), m.scale*(1+0.08*s)) (squash forward on impact); lunge(lerp(-0.20,0.45,s)) — biggest lunge of all types (a darting strike). onHit at u>=0.45.
perType.recovery: u 0.50..1.0 (r=easeOutCubic(remap)): recoil/peel away. torso.rotation.x = lerp(0.55,0,r); head.rotation.x = lerp(0.45,0,r); arms.x = lerp(1.2,0.5,r); g.scale -> m.scale; lunge(0) at end (snaps back to its hover position so the next bob/weave reads). Quick so it darts in and out.
perType.lunge: recoil 0.20m then a long +0.45m dart-in on the bite, fully restored on recovery so it returns to its hovering circle pattern. The big lunge + stretch->squash sells the spirit pounce on a creature with no real weapon.

perType.type: shaman — staff-raise channel (arcane bolt) + hunched beam-channel (close hex). NOT atkPhase-driven; reuse the EXISTING m.casting/T.castWind window and m.beaming flag, just enrich the poses. Owns arms.L, arms.R, torso, head; staff stays on g (does not swing).
perType.windup: BOLT channel (while m.casting>0): cc = 1 - Math.max(0,m.casting)/T.castWind (0..1 charge meter, already computed for the crystal emissive). arms.R.rotation.x = lerp(0.5,-1.1,easeInOut(cc)) (raise staff arm high overhead — deeper than the current -0.9); arms.L.rotation.x = lerp(0.5,-0.7,easeInOut(cc)) (off-hand rises too, two-handed cast); torso.rotation.x = lerp(0,-0.20,cc) (slight back-lean, upright regal); head.rotation.x = lerp(0,-0.15,cc) (look up the staff); keep the existing crystal emissiveIntensity ramp 1.4..3.6 as the visible charge meter. This held upright staff-raise pose IS the bolt telegraph.
perType.strike: BOLT release (frame m.casting<=0, where shamanCast(m) already fires): snap arms.R.rotation.x to 1.0 via lerp(cur,1.0,0.4) for ~3 frames (a quick downward staff-point thrust toward the player as the bolt leaves), torso.rotation.x->0.15, then let the positioning branch ease arms back to 0.5. No new damage timing — shamanCast is unchanged.
perType.recovery: after a cast, the existing else-branch (m.casting=0) and the d<5/d>castRange positioning idle (arms 0.5+sin(t*2)*0.15) naturally eases the arms back; add torso.rotation.x=lerp(torso.rotation.x,0,0.1) and head.rotation.x=lerp(...,0,0.1) in that idle branch so the lean settles.
perType.lunge: none for the bolt (stationary upright cast). BEAM mode (while m.beaming, the close hex): hold a distinct HUNCHED-FORWARD pose instead — torso.rotation.x=lerp(torso.rotation.x,0.30,0.15) (lean in menacingly), head.rotation.x=lerp(...,0.25,0.15) (head down), arms.L.rotation.x=arms.R.rotation.x=lerp(...,0.9,0.15) (both arms forward & low, staff thrust at player), held continuously while m.beaming is true. A tiny lunge(0.12) toward the player optional. This sustained hunch is visually distinct from the upright bolt cast and reads as a sustained close-range drain.

perType.type: spitter — lurch-back then heave (lobs the ebomb). NOT atkPhase-driven; wrap the EXISTING m.rangedTimer fire. Add a charge window: when m.rangedTimer drops below T.castTell (e.g. 0.5s) begin the back-lurch BEFORE the fire frame. Owns torso, head, arms.L, arms.R, the belly sac scale, lunge. Belly = the silhouette anchor, so inflate it as the tell.
perType.windup: CHARGE (m.rangedTimer in (0, 0.5]): cw = easeInOut(1 - m.rangedTimer/0.5) (0..1). torso.rotation.x = lerp(0,-0.40,cw) (lurch the whole torso BACK); head.rotation.x = lerp(0,-0.25,cw) (throw head back); arms.L.rotation.x=arms.R.rotation.x=lerp(0.3,-0.5,cw) (little arms rear back to cradle the lob); INFLATE belly: if you cache the main bile-sac mesh on m.bellySac in build(), m.bellySac.scale.setScalar(1+0.10*cw) (~8-10% swell = visible charging); lunge(lerp(0,-0.20,cw)) (whole toad rocks back 0.2m). This back-lurch + bulging belly telegraphs the incoming lob.
perType.strike: HEAVE (frame m.rangedTimer<=0, where spitterFire(m) already fires): snap forward — torso.rotation.x=lerp(cur,0.50,0.5) for ~3-4 frames (violent forward heave); head.rotation.x->0.30; arms.L.rotation.x=arms.R.rotation.x=lerp(cur,1.2,0.5) (throw the lob); DEFLATE belly m.bellySac.scale.setScalar(lerp(cur,1.0,0.5)) (snaps back as the bile is expelled); lunge(lerp(cur,0.18,0.5)) (heave forward). spitterFire timing unchanged.
perType.recovery: after fire (back in the bandMin/bandMax idle branch where arms are set to 0.3): ease the heave out — torso.rotation.x=lerp(torso.rotation.x,0,0.12); head.rotation.x=lerp(...,0,0.12); m.bellySac.scale -> 1.0; lunge folded back. The existing idle arm-set (0.3) is the resting toad pose.
perType.lunge: rock back 0.20m during the charge, +0.18m forward heave on the fire frame, restored as it settles back into its keep-distance band. Slow telegraphed lurch-charge-heave timing = the toad's ponderous personality.

resetRule:
CLEAN COMPOSITION WITH WALK: each ATK pose fn writes ABSOLUTE values only to the joints it owns; while m.atkPhase>0 the case body takes the attack branch and never calls walkAnim(), so walk cannot fight the swing. Joints an attack does NOT touch (e.g. legs for grunt/shard, torso for scout's x) are simply left at their last walk value — harmless because they're small and walk resumes writing them next idle frame. On the LAST frame endAtk() sets m.atkPhase=0; the next non-attack frame, walkAnim() (when moving) or the cooldown guard (arms->0.5 lerp at the top of meleeAttack) eases owned joints back to baseline. Add an explicit settle-to-baseline at endAtk for joints walk does NOT own: torso.rotation.x=0, torso.rotation.z=0, head.rotation.x=0, and g.scale.set(m.scale,m.scale,m.scale) — do these via a short lerp over the recovery sub-phase (already specified per type) so there is no snap, and hard-set them in endAtk as a safety net.

LUNGE SAFETY: the lunge writes p.x/p.z as lungeBase + (dx/d)*amt every frame, so it never compounds; endAtk restores p.x/p.z to lungeBase and nulls it, so a creature can't drift away on a stale lunge vector. moveToward is NOT called during the attack branch, so the lunge and pathing never fight. (For wisp, restore lungeBase = its current hover anchor so the bob/weave resumes cleanly.)

DEATH SAFETY (critical): in killMonster (line 2382, right after m.alive=false; m.dying=0.001;) add: if(m.lungeBase){ m.g.position.x=m.lungeBase.x; m.g.position.z=m.lungeBase.z; m.lungeBase=null; } m.atkPhase=0; m.atkKind=null; m.atkHit=false; — this restores any mid-lunge position and clears the attack so the death switch (which owns arms/legs/head/g.rotation/g.scale/g.position from frame one) starts from a clean transform and a mid-swing pose can't fight the topple/burst/dissolve. Also reset torso.rotation.set(0,0,0) and head.rotation.x=0 there since the death anims assume torso isn't pre-rotated (they only animate head/arms/legs/g, never torso). Because m.dying>0 makes update() take the death branch and skip trollAI entirely, no attack code runs after death-start.

CO-OP / FROZEN SAFETY: keep everything inside trollAI which already early-returns for coopIsRemote(m) (remote trolls only walk) and for m.rooted>0/m.frozenSolid>0 (which already lerps arms.R to 0.5) — so attacks are purely local cosmetic and don't desync; the existing onHit->damagePlayer calls fire at the same logical moment as before (just at the strike sub-frame instead of immediately), so co-op damage timing is unchanged. If m.rooted/frozenSolid triggers mid-swing, the existing early-return leaves m.atkPhase as-is; add m.atkPhase=0 + lunge-restore to that early-return block too so a paralyzed troll doesn't freeze in a half-lunge.

globalPolish: SPLIT embellishTroll into two passes. Today (lines 1547-1573) every troll gets spine-crest + bone horns + 5 moss/lichen blobs + 3 warts + 2-3 glow mushrooms + a chest rune sprite. That homogenizes silhouettes and competes with each type's anchor. Make a LIGHT shared pass embellishTroll(m) that adds ONLY the Mossfolk family signature: the 5 mossMat/lichenMat clumps stay BUT pull them off the silhouette edge into the back/shoulder mass (keep x within +/-0.34, push z to -0.30..-0.46 so they don't break the outline), drop glow mushrooms from 2-3 to exactly 1 small spore on the back (sprout sc 0.55, opacity 0.5), keep the 3 warts (skinDark, interior texture). MOVE the bone horns and the tall back-crest OUT of the shared pass entirely (they currently fire for grunt/scout/brute/spitter/shaman and stack on top of type-specific crowns/pauldrons -- pure noise). Let each build() own its hero silhouette piece. Net: shared pass goes from ~16 meshes to ~9, and no two types share a crown shape anymore.

REMOVE the chest RUNA sprite from the shared pass (line 1572, additive cyan GLOW at y1.05,z0.5 scale 0.5). It's an additive cyan patch identical to the game's ice/heal/spark VFX color family (0x36c5ff) on EVERY troll -- it reads as VFX noise floating on the chest, adds nothing to the cutout, and muddies element-tint reads. Delete it for non-shaman/non-wisp types; the shaman's staff crystal and the wisp's core already carry the 'arcane glow' read where it belongs.

VALUE-CONTRAST PASS on the three shared materials so any element tint survives. Push bellyMat (line 255, currently 0x9ec089 -- a green) toward a true bone-cream near-white 0xd9d2bc so it reads as the LIGHT value anchor (it's the under-belly/inner-ear/throat rim plane). Darken mossMat's role as the near-BLACK anchor is fine as-is (0x3c5a3e), but lower its roughness from 1 to ~0.92 so flat-shaded facets catch a faint highlight step. Keep tuskMat (0xeee2cf) as the second near-white. Result: mid-skin (tinted) sits between a dark moss mass and two near-white masses (bone+belly) that elementSkin never recolors -- the form holds under fire/ice/poison/lightning tint.

ADD jitter() to the carved-stone masses that don't have it yet so flat-shading earns its free facet lighting. The brute pauldrons/hump already jitter; extend it to: the shard's TetrahedronGeometry rubble (line 1706, wrap in jitter 0.04,0.04), the spitter is organic so leave its spheres smooth. This is the cheapest 'carved rock vs untextured prototype' upgrade -- zero new meshes, just non-coplanar facets.

GEOMETRIC RIM PLANE convention: give every type ONE pale (bellyMat, the new bone-cream) up/out-facing facet on its hero feature so it catches light and separates from the dark forest without a glow shader. Scout already does this (inner-ear membrane). Generalize: brute = lit top plane on each pauldron; spitter = pale underside ridge on the belly (it has one, keep it); grunt = pale throat wedge under the jaw; shaman = pale inner-antler facet; shard = pale chip on one crystal face; wisp = the bone face-plate (see wisp entry). This is the single biggest 'sculpted not prototype' tell and it's element-tint-proof.

Keep emissiveIntensity on bodySkin <= 0.3 for all non-wisp types (elementSkin sets it from C.emInt at line 1578 -- verify no element preset exceeds ~0.3). High emissive flattens the free flat-shading shadow gradient and makes a tinted troll glow as a featureless blob. Only the wisp (core/eyes), shaman (crystal/horns), and shard (crack seams) should carry real emissive.

perType.proportion: It's the readability baseline so keep it a balanced humanoid, but commit it slightly: postureLean 0.05 -> 0.12 (a small forward brawler hunch), shoulderW 1.0 -> 1.12, torsoW 1.0 -> 1.05, neckLen 0.0 -> -0.03, scaleMul 1.0 stays. The club IS its anchor shape on the silhouette edge (right fist), so the body stays neutral on purpose -- it's the 'default troll' the others read against.
perType.heroDetails: (1) REFINE the existing club rather than add: keep the hafted stone-wood club (lines 1607-1623) but enlarge the striking head burl from Dodeca(0.20) to Dodeca(0.24) and run jitter(geo,0.05,0.05) on it (already partially done) so it reads as one bold carved mass on the outline at 30m instead of a fuzzy lump; angle club.rotation more out-of-knuckles (rotation.x -0.35 -> -0.20) so the head clears the body outline. (2) ADD a single pale throat wedge: mesh(new THREE.SphereGeometry(0.22,9,6), bellyMat, 0,-0.14,0.22, 1.3,0.5,0.9) parented to m.head as the geometric rim plane under the jaw -- one mesh, catches light, survives any tint.
perType.palette: Lean fully on the material trinity: skin (tintable), mossMat crown + back (dark anchor, keep the extra crown moss at lines 1625-1626), bellyMat throat/under-belly (new bone-cream light anchor). Club stays woodMat/woodDark so it reads as 'carried object' value, not skin. No new std() material.
perType.remove: Remove the shared-pass bone HORNS and the tall back-CREST from this type (they currently stack on the grunt and clutter its clean brawler outline). Remove the chest rune sprite. Keep the crown moss and the 3 warts.

perType.proportion: Push the lean inverted-triangle HARDER: postureLean 0.32 -> 0.40 (deep forward crouch-lean of a sprinter), shoulderW 0.78 stays, torsoW 0.72 -> 0.66 (narrower gut so the shoulders/ears dominate), neckLen 0.0 -> 0.06 (slight neck poke-forward, predator), scaleMul 0.62 stays. Keep the limb-thinning Group scales (line 1636). The 0.85 bat-ears remain THE giveaway on the cutout.
perType.heroDetails: (1) Keep + sharpen the bat-ears: they're the anchor. Lengthen earLen 0.85 -> 0.95 and SWEEP them back (the inner-ear cone at line 1642-1643 already adds the pale bellyMat membrane -- keep it, it's the model rim-plane other types should copy). Add a tiny notch: a small skinDark ConeGeometry(0.03,0.08,4) cut into one ear tip for a torn-ear scavenger read (1 mesh). (2) Keep the forearm bone daggers (lines 1660-1666) -- they're the 'armed & fast' silhouette accent on the arm edge; just confirm they ride the arm Group so the new twin-claw swipe swings them.
perType.palette: Trinity only: tinted skin, ONE sparse moss tuft over an ear (line 1670, keep), bone daggers + inner-ear in bone/belly light value. The scout wears the LEAST moss of the caste by design -- do not add more.
perType.remove: Remove the shared-pass HORNS and back-CREST entirely (a quick imp should be smooth-skulled, not crested -- the crest currently fights the ear read badly). Remove the chest rune. Keep the snaggle fangs (lines 1648-1656).

perType.type: brute 'Kobunko' (huge tank, splits into 2 shard on death)
perType.proportion: Maximize the triangle-on-legs wedge: neckLen -0.10 -> -0.14 and head.scale 0.82 -> 0.78 (head buried deeper between shoulders), shoulderW 1.5 -> 1.6, torsoW 1.35 -> 1.42, postureLean 0.18 -> 0.22, scaleMul 1.7 stays. The boulder pauldrons + back-hump already make the wide granite mass -- the smaller head amplifies it.
perType.heroDetails: (1) Add the bellyMat LIT TOP PLANE on each pauldron (the rim-light convention): after each pauldron dome (line 1683) add mesh(new THREE.DodecahedronGeometry(0.30,0), bellyMat, p[0],p[1]+0.16,p[2], 1.0,0.35,1.0) jittered -- a flat light-catching cap so the granite shoulders read as sculpted stone, not dark blobs, under any tint (2 meshes, one per shoulder). (2) Keep the two-handed maul + the broken splayed tusks (lines 1692-1699) -- the maul is the side-silhouette anchor. Run jitter on the maul head (line 1694) if not already, and widen the tusk splay rotation.z s*0.28 -> s*0.40 so they break the jaw outline.
perType.palette: Trinity holds beautifully here: tinted grey-green skin, mossMat-dark pauldrons/hump (near-black masses), new bellyMat caps + woodMat stone nubs + tuskMat tusks (near-white). The 'two coals in shadow' brow-moss crust (line 1701) stays -- it's the eye-recess framing the art direction calls for.
perType.remove: Remove the shared-pass thin bone HORNS and the tall back-CREST -- the brute's silhouette is owned by the stone pauldrons + hump + maul; thin horns/crest on top look fussy and undercut the heavy mineral read. Remove the chest rune.

perType.type: shaman 'Saman' (two-mode caster: arcane bolt + close hex beam)
perType.proportion: Tall narrow spire crowned by the antler sweep: it has good bones already (limb-thin scales line 1716, head lengthen 0.92,1.12,0.95, neck lift). Push neckLen 0.15 -> 0.20 and add a slight torsoW 0.85 -> 0.80 to make the spire even narrower so the antler crown is the widest point of the silhouette (a spire topped by a sweep). postureLean stays ~0.0 (upright priest). scaleMul 1.05 stays.
perType.heroDetails: (1) The antler-horn crown IS the anchor (lines 1719-1726) -- keep it but make it sweep WIDER and more clearly back: horn.rotation.z s*0.62 -> s*0.72 and add one more rear tine per side so the crown silhouette reads as a distinct branching shape, not just two spikes (2 meshes total, one tine per side). Add a pale bellyMat inner facet on each main horn so the crown catches light (fold into the rim-plane convention). (2) Keep the crystal staff (lines 1733-1744) -- it's the side-anchor and the cast animation prop. The staff crystal stays the pulsing emissive focal point.
perType.palette: Here the ONE hero accent material is justified: the shared staffMat (teal emissive, line 1712) drives crown + staff + brow gem + runes -- that's the 'mystic' value that survives tinting because it's a fixed teal-emissive, not skin. Keep emissive on the crystal only as the pulsing charge-meter; keep horns at a lower emissiveIntensity so they read as shape first. Bone-bead necklace (line 1731) stays as the bone-white throat accent.
perType.remove: Remove the shared-pass generic bone HORNS (they'd stack on the antler crown -- double horns) and the shared back-CREST (the antler sweep is the crown). Remove the shared chest rune (the staff/crystal already carries arcane glow). Keep the shoulder moss-mantle (line 1747).

perType.proportion: Bottom-heavy teardrop -- the sagging belly IS the silhouette: keep the thick-stubby-leg / thin-arm scaling (line 1764), push torsoW 1.25 -> 1.32, postureLean 0.10 -> 0.16 (leans over the gut), neckLen -0.05 stays, scaleMul 1.05 stays. The belly sac (line 1767) should be the visual center of mass low and forward so the cutout reads as a teardrop balanced on stubby legs.
perType.heroDetails: (1) The huge bile-belly is the anchor (lines 1766-1769) -- keep it; the pale bellyMat highlight ridge (line 1769) is already the up-facing rim plane, keep it. Make the main sac slightly more pendulous: lower it y0.86 -> y0.80 and push z 0.30 -> 0.36 so it overhangs the legs on the outline. (2) Keep the wide frog-mouth + puffed throat sac (lines 1779-1786) -- the second anchor; the throat inflates in the lob wind-up. Keep the single drip-bead (gross detail). These already exist; no net-new hero mesh needed -- spend the budget on the belly tweak only.
perType.palette: The per-monster spitMat bile material (line 1761, olive emissive 0.35) is the justified hero accent -- it ties belly + throat + back-pustules into one diseased value. Keep skin tintable, mossMat caps on the belly/back so it still reads Mossfolk (lines 1771,1777), bellyMat ridge as the light plane. Verify spitMat emissiveIntensity stays ~0.35 (under the 0.3-ish ceiling is fine for a non-skin accent).
perType.remove: Remove the shared-pass bone HORNS and back-CREST -- they sit on top of the oozing back-pustule cluster (lines 1773-1775) and clutter the diseased-toad read. The pustule cluster IS this type's back signature. Remove the chest rune (the glowing belly is the focal glow).

perType.type: wisp 'Liderc' (the unnoticed spirit -- RESOLVE into a readable creature)
perType.proportion: Make it person-sized and slower so it dwells in view: scaleMul 0.7 -> 0.95, speedMul 1.55 -> 1.2, speedCap 6.0 -> 4.5, hpMul 0.6 -> 1.0 (survives one charged arrow), and flip resist.fire 1.5 -> 1.0 so a fire arrow no longer one-shots it before it's perceived; keep ice 0.35 for theme. postureLean small. The body opacity goes from translucent-particle to a SOLID core: set m.bodySkin.transparent=true but opacity 0.6 -> 0.92 (line 1594) so the rig silhouette reads as a creature.
perType.heroDetails: (1) OPAQUE WRAITH HOOD/SHROUD as the anchor: add a non-additive MeshStandardMaterial cloth=std({color:0x0e2c38,emissive:0x1b6f8c,emissiveIntensity:0.5,flatShading:true}); then a ConeGeometry(0.55,1.6,7) centered ~y1.3 over torso+head plus 2 jagged lower cones (ConeGeometry(0.22,0.7,6) at the hem) -- a tattered dark-teal hooded mantle that reads as a clear opaque spirit shape against its own glow. (2) BONE FACE-PLATE + 2 bright eyes: a small opaque bellyMat/bone mask plane inside the hood (SphereGeometry(0.16,8,6) flattened) carrying the two emissive eye spheres so there's an unmistakable face inside the shroud -- this is THE 'creature not particle' fix. (3) 2 curved wraith horns (reuse horn code with cloth material) for a distinct skull shape.
perType.palette: Cut the additive-glow dominance so the body wins: core sprite opacity 0.85 -> 0.45 and scale 1.2 -> 0.8 (line 1597); floating sparks 5 -> 2 and opacity 0.5 -> 0.3 (line 1598). The dark-teal opaque cloth (mossMat-role near-black) + bone face-plate (near-white) + the two bright eyes as the focal point = the trinity restored for a spirit. The glow becomes a HALO around a solid body, not the body itself.
perType.remove: Remove 3 of the 5 floating spark sprites and shrink the core (they mimic the game's own VFX particle system and make the brain edit it out as ambient FX). Stop letting embellishTroll early-return with nothing (line 1548): give it the 3-4 opaque silhouette features above instead of moss (it's a spirit, no moss), so it has a distinct cutout under any tint -- right now it has the least-distinctive, most-transparent silhouette of all 7 types.

perType.type: shard 'Koszilank' (small rubble troll from brute split)
perType.proportion: Reads as a jagged broken-off chunk: postureLean 0.16 stays, shoulderW 1.15, torsoW 1.1, neckLen -0.06, scaleMul 0.62 stays. Keep it asymmetric and angular -- it should look like a piece of the brute, all hard mineral planes, not a smooth small troll.
perType.heroDetails: (1) The glowing orange crack seams are the anchor (lines 1704-1705) -- keep the _crackMat BoxGeometry seams. ADD 1-2 angular shard-plates jutting off the back/shoulder: mesh(jitter(new THREE.IcosahedronGeometry(0.22,0),0.06,0.06), woodMat, 0.18,1.3,-0.25) -- a sharp rock slab on the outline so the cutout reads 'broken stone' not 'small troll' (1-2 meshes). (2) Add a single pale chip facet (bellyMat) on one shard plate as the rim-light plane. Run jitter() on the existing 3 tetrahedron rubble bits (line 1706) so no facet is coplanar -- the 'carved rock' trick.
perType.palette: Grey stone trinity: tintable grey skin, woodMat/woodDark stone slabs (warm rock value), tuskMat/bellyMat chips (near-white). The crack-seam emissive (orange 0xffb24d) is the justified hero accent and the only glow -- it ties the shard to the brute's fiery split death.
perType.remove: Note: shard currently has NO embellishTroll skip and NO build-added horns/crest of its own beyond rubble -- but the shared pass DOES add horns+crest+moss to it. Remove the shared bone HORNS and tall back-CREST (a rock fragment shouldn't have organic horns), and reduce shared moss to 1-2 clumps (it's mostly stone). Remove the chest rune.
